description: Outcome-level upstream-vs-Ultra behavioral comparison cases. Each case describes the upstream behavior expectation, Ultra's observed/implemented behavior, and the Rust evidence that makes the comparison auditable.

cases.upstream_behavior: Interactive slash commands and command aliases remain discoverable and route to the expected CLI/TUI handlers.
cases.ultra_behavior: Rust CLI command aliases and local slash-query routing cover the upstream golden command surface while preserving terminal-first behavior.
cases.why: This is behavioral symmetry: the same operator intents are reachable through the Rust command surface.

cases.upstream_behavior: Web research requests can use search/extract/crawl tools and return readable source snippets to the agent surface.
cases.ultra_behavior: Rust toolsets expose web_search, web_extract, and web_crawl, and ACP formatting converts web results into compact terminal-facing evidence.
cases.why: This proves the same web-research tool loop exists in Rust rather than only counting source files.

cases.upstream_behavior: The agent retrieves durable context before solving tasks when memory context is relevant.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra explicitly prefers ContextLattice as a Rust-native memory backbone, injects the active topic into runtime guidance, autopins objective topics, and pressure-tunes retrieval mode under queue load.
cases.why: This is stricter than behavioral parity because the memory loop has a preferred ContextLattice backbone and pressure-aware retrieval controls.

cases.upstream_behavior: For repository work, the agent should inspect local evidence, gather relevant project context, and avoid plan-only answers when implementation is requested.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra loads workspace context files, scans context for prompt-injection risk, and injects runtime reformulation that requires ContextLattice-first grounding plus concrete action.
cases.why: This directly targets repo-research planning quality rather than counting test coverage rows.

cases.upstream_behavior: Hard questions should be synthesized from available evidence and disagreements should be surfaced instead of fabricated away.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra adds a quorum synthesis path with independent voter outputs, disagreement handling, thin-evidence warnings, and explicit bans on invented files, commands, metrics, and citations.
cases.why: This is an outcome-quality extension over a single-pass answer loop.

cases.upstream_behavior: Provider auth, OAuth refresh, model routing, and provider aliases should recover cleanly from stale credentials and route to the selected provider.
cases.ultra_behavior: Rust auth and routing cover upstream provider aliases, core OAuth refreshable providers, provider-specific API modes, and Nous stale singleton repair.
cases.why: This keeps interactive model/provider behavior aligned with upstream while using Rust config and auth stores.

cases.upstream_behavior: The agent can run against common provider backends through OpenAI-compatible request/response semantics.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra expands Rust-native local OpenAI-compatible backend support across llama.cpp, MLX, LM Studio, LMDeploy, LocalAI, KoboldCpp, text-generation-webui, and TabbyAPI aliases with no-key local/private defaults.
cases.why: This is a wider local model runtime surface than upstream Python parity alone.

cases.upstream_behavior: Gateway adapters enforce DM/group allowlists and provider-specific platform authorization behavior.
cases.ultra_behavior: Rust gateway policy resolves platform allowlists, group authorization, Telegram chat allowlists, and WhatsApp LID mapping with explicit tests.
cases.why: This compares the user-visible authorization outcome, not just adapter file presence.

cases.upstream_behavior: Scheduled jobs and platform deliveries preserve the intended destination and expose useful completion output.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra preserves Telegram group/private topics, applies cron thread routing at fire time, and delivers full cron output while supporting silent suppression.
cases.why: This is stricter platform behavior than a generic scheduled-message parity check.

cases.upstream_behavior: Skills can be discovered, installed, audited, and used without silently accepting unsafe or malformed content.
cases.ultra_behavior: Rust skills governance merges official taps, verifies skill trust tiers, scans bundles before install, and preserves upstream-compatible tap/subscription shapes.
cases.why: This adds explicit governance and audit behavior around upstream skill surfaces.

cases.upstream_behavior: Python plugin/test behavior should not leak into production runtime as an untracked dependency.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra keeps upstream Python plugin and test trees reference-only, proves Rust-owned behavior through parity manifests, and runs a no-runtime-Python guard before merge.
cases.why: This is a deliberate runtime-safety improvement over direct Python vendoring.

cases.upstream_behavior: Protocol adapters and runtime loops should behave deterministically under transport failures, malformed tool payloads, auth expiry, and stream interruptions.
cases.ultra_behavior: Ultra has a SOTA harness matrix with workflow replay, protocol differential contracts, and chaos scenarios covering timeouts, 5xx, rate limits, connection resets, auth expiry, malformed tool payloads, and partial stream drops.
cases.why: This turns resilience into a measured behavior surface rather than relying on happy-path parity.
